title: Mission to Nar Shaddaa (5 ABY)
content:
{{Top|leg}} {{Citation}} {{Conjecture|legends}} {{Otheruses|Mission to Nar Shaddaa}} {{Mission |prev=[[Mission to Dorlo]]''[[Star Wars: Jedi Knight: Dark Forces II]]'' |conc=[[Milagro campaign]]''[[The New Essential Chronology]]'' |next=*[[Mission to Sulon]] *[[Battle of Fort Nowhere]] |image=[[File:Katarn_meets_8t88.jpg]] |name=Mission to Nar Shaddaa |conflict=[[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]]''[[Dark Forces: Rebel Agent]]'' |objective=Capture and decrypt [[Katarn family]] [[Hologram disc/Legends|holodisc]] |date=[[5 ABY/Legends|5 ABY]]''[[The Essential Reader's Companion]]'' |place=[[Nar Shaddaa/Legends|Nar Shaddaa]] |result=Holodisc retrieved, escape of 8t88 |side1=[[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]] agents |side2=*[[8t88]]'s forces *[[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]] |side3= |side4= |commanders1= |commanders2= |commanders3= |commanders4= |unit1= |unit2= |unit3= |unit4= |ppl1=*[[Kyle Katarn]] *[[Jan Ors]] |ppl2=*8t88 *2 [[Bodyguard/Legends|bodyguards]] **[[Grentho]] *[[Bounty hunter/Legends|Bounty hunters]] **[[Porg (bounty hunter)|Porg]] **An [[Unidentified Aqualish (Nar Shaddaa)|Aqualish bounty hunter]] **A [[Unidentified Gran (Rimmer's Rest)|Gran bounty hunter]] **A [[Unidentified bounty hunter (Rimmer's Rest)|Grave Tusken bounty hunter]] **A [[Unidentified Rodian bounty hunter (Nar Shaddaa)|Rodian bounty hunter]] *[[Gamorrean/Legends|Gamorrean]] [[Security guard/Legends|guards]] *[[Stormtrooper/Legends|Stormtroopers]] *[[TIE pilot]]s *1 [[TIE/sa tactical bomber/Legends|TIE bomber]]†''[[Star Wars: Jedi Knight: Dark Forces II]]'' |ppl3= |ppl4= |casual1=None |casual2= *Both bodyguards **Grentho *Several bounty hunters **Porg **An [[Aqualish/Legends|Aqualish]] bounty hunter *1 TIE bomber |casual3= |casual4= |civilian= }} {{Quote|You'll never leave here alive, Katarn. Nar Shaddaa will be your grave!"
"I'm not interested in leaving; not until I conclude my business with 88.|A Gran bounty hunter and Kyle Katarn|}} On his '''second mission to Nar Shaddaa''', [[Kyle Katarn]] sought information on his [[Parent/Legends|father]] [[Morgan Katarn]]'s [[Jerec (Inquisitor)|killer]], for which he met with [[Droid/Legends|droid]] [[Information broker/Legends|information broker]] [[8t88/Legends|8t88]], who claimed knowledge of the killer's identity, in the [[Rimmer's Rest/Legends|Rimmer's Rest]] [[Cantina/Legends|bar]]. The droid, however, was in the employ of the elder Katarn's killer, the [[Dark Jedi/Legends|Dark Jedi]] Jerec, and, backed by hired [[Bodyguard/Legends|bodyguards]] and [[Bounty hunter/Legends|bounty hunters]], had lured the younger Katarn there in the hope of accessing an encrypted [[Hologram disc/Legends|holodisc]] which he had found in the [[Katarn family compound|Katarn family's homestead]]. ==Background== In [[5 ABY/Legends|5 ABY]], [[Information broker/Legends|infochant]] [[Droid/Legends|droid]] [[8t88]] was hired to assist the [[Dark Jedi/Legends|Dark Jedi]] [[Jerec (Inquisitor)|Jerec]] in locating the elusive [[Valley of the Jedi]]. His search led him to the abandoned [[Katarn estate]] on [[Sulon/Legends|Sulon]], the former residence of [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebel]] leader [[Morgan Katarn]], [[Death/Legends|executed]] by Jerec himself in [[1 BBY/Legends|1 BBY]]. 8t88 salvaged several [[Hologram disc/Legends|holodiscs]] but was unable to decipher one. For this he lured Katarn's son, [[Kyle Katarn]], who was seeking knowledge on the identity of his father's killer, and arranged a meeting. Receiving his message, Katarn was eager to meet the information broker at the [[Rimmer's Rest/Legends|Rimmer's Rest]] [[Cantina/Legends|cantina]] on [[Nar Shaddaa/Legends|Nar Shaddaa]]. His partner, [[Jan Ors]], sensed it was a ruse and attempted to dissuade him. Katarn pretended to listen to her concerns but while she wasn't expecting it, he left the Alliance headquarters on his own. Ors followed his trace on the ''[[Moldy Crow/Legends|Moldy Crow]]'', knowing that he would need to be saved by her one more time. Meanwhile the droid, fearing that Katarn would refuse to assist him in cracking the holodisk, at first hoped to recruit the [[Bounty hunter/Legends|bounty hunter]] [[Boba Fett/Legends|Boba Fett]] to act as a bodyguard, who declined. The droid made due with [[Unidentified Gran (Rimmer's Rest)|several]] [[Unidentified bounty hunter (Rimmer's Rest)|other]] [[Alien/Legends|alien]] thugs instead. ==Chasing 8t88== Indeed, a little later Katarn met with the droid who started giving the information Katarn wanted, revealing the identity of Jerec, whom Katarn himself had met a few years ealier during his graduation. 88 was then joined by his hirelings who surrounded Katarn; the droid offered Kyle his life in exchange for decoding his father's holodisc but the Rebel agent refused to cooperate. 8t88 ordered the man's death and left. [[File:Narshadaahrushhour.jpg|thumb|left|160px|Kyle Katarn chasing 8t88 through the streets of Nar Shaddaa]] Kyle Katarn however had released a [[tracker droid]] that attached itself to 8t88. Kyle Katarn was left in the company of two hirelings who took his [[Bryar pistol/Legends|Bryar pistol]] and were ready to kill him; but Katarn somehow managing to cause a blaster to fly through the air and straight into his own hands—unknowingly, he had performed a [[Telekinesis/Legends|Force Pull]]—and easily overcame them. Following his tracker's signals, he went after 8t88. Kyle Katarn had to fight his way to the top levels of Nar Shaddaa, having to shoot his way through 8t88's minions. Katarn reached the top just as 8t88 was about board ''[[Furious (Lambda-class)|Furious]]''. Katarn fired at 8t88, shooting off the arm in which the droid was holding the holodisc. The limb, still clenching the holodisc dropped deep into the bowels of Nar Shaddaa, while 8t88 got away in his ship. Katarn exchanged a volley of shots with 8t88's guards including [[Grentho]]. All of a sudden, a [[TIE/sa tactical bomber/Legends|TIE bomber]] appeared, ready to destroy Katarn. Fortunately, Jan Ors, who was looking for Katarn, also appeared in the ''Moldy Crow'' and blasted the TIE bomber into pieces, saving Katarn's life. ==Air battle== After leaving the tower and [[Launch Deck Three]], Jan Ors in the ''Crow'' was pursued by two [[TIE/ln space superiority starfighter/Legends|TIE/LN starfighters]]. Using her high piloting skills she attempted to gain distance in the artificial "canyons", and putting building spires between her and her pursuers, all while Kyle was fighting 8t88's minions. The [[TIE pilot]]s, displaying their arrogant neglect of citizen life (or even desperation) and flew and shot among the inhabited buildings, demolishing a sky bridge in the process, which fell into the canyon, along with the people on it. To end the vicious zigzagging, Jan hid the ''Crow'' in the empty rectangular space of a spire under construction without walls; displaying her piloting skills in putting the ship down in the right amount of speed, without navigational lights. Although the TIEs swept past her location, one of them detected her engine's heat and returned; Jan shot it down once it came in front of her, blocking her way out. While the other was following up, Jan flew the ''Crow'' vertically through the future [[Turbolift/Legends|turbolift]] shaft of the unfinished building, emerged from the top and and circled down as the TIE was blasting away. Her cannons caused it to crash into the building. Jan reached a platform waiting for Kyle but because of the high fees, and the arrival of other ships, she had to leave; for the following three hours she returned attempting to spot Kyle and in vain to contact him over the radio. ==Into the bowels of Nar Shaddaa== Kyle Katarn had no choice but to descend into the depths of the lower levels of the Smugglers' Moon to retrieve it. He and Ors agreed on a rendezvous point where she would pick him up, once he had found the holodisc. He climbed down a maintenance ladder to the lower levels, but an Imperial patrol ship located him with its searchlight and its cannons opened fire. Between the ship and a [[Maintenance droid/Legends|maintenance droid]] sent to knock him clear; Katarn made a sideway leap to a broken apartment window and the droid wailed as it roared by. Katarn climbed into the broken apartment; it was when Kyle exited the door into the hall when the ship started firing again, devastating the apartment. Pursued by Imperials, Katarn descended a fire escape, then tubeways, and reached the streets, even long-forgotten levels of the ecumenopolis where the heat was stronger. Fighting his way through the sewers, as 8t88 had left several thugs behind to capture him, Katarn managed to find 8t88's severed arm thanks to a [[Glowrod/Legends|glow rod]], the hand still holding the disc. He was surprised by the [[Trandoshan/Legends|Trandoshan]] [[Mercenary/Legends|mercenary]] [[Porg (bounty hunter)|Porg]], who managed to disarm him. Katarn evaded his blow and jumped to grab the droid arm, which he used to block his second blow. He temporarily incapacitated the Trandoshan when an [[Unidentified Aqualish (Nar Shaddaa)|Aqualish]] bounty hunter joined. Kyle turned off his rod and once more used telekinesis to retrieve his [[Bryar pistol/Legends|Bryar pistol]], exchanging fire with the [[Aqualish/Legends|Aqualish]] where both he and Porg were killed. Hearing more pursuers coming, Kyle Katarn passed through a cemetery and reached a vertical spiraling ramp that connected the mines of the surface to the loading docks far above. Following the ore for some time, he jumped to a cross brace to avoid a machinery. Seeing his pursuers climbing a ladder, he walked a beam in order to climb aboard a catwalk, which led to a maintenance platform and a freight lift, which led to roof access. On his way up he also climbed about thirty stairs and a dozen of ladders. ==Skirmish on the deck== [[File:Ouch.jpg|thumb|left|160px|Kyle Katarn gets seriously injured.]] He made it back to the top level, where he was to rendezvous with Jan Ors in the ''Moldy Crow''. But fifteen thugs waited for him there, and a blast bolt penetrated his shoulder. Exhausted, Katarn was on the brink of giving up, when suddenly a voice, which came from [[Qu Rahn/Legends|Qu Rahn's]] [[Force ghost/Legends|spirit]] spoke to him telling him to let [[The Force/Legends|the Force]] protect him as he went to peace within. It was as if suddenly he had oceans of time, and with great ease, he managed to shoot seven[[Dark Forces: Rebel Agent (audio drama)|''Dark Forces: Rebel Agent'' audio drama]] of his pursuers, a [[Rodian/Legends|Rodian]], a [[Gamorrean/Legends|Gamorrean]] and a [[Human/Legends|human]]. Kyle had used the [[Force power/Legends|Force power]] [[Force speed/Legends|Force speed]] for the very first time. Feeling a sense of pride and invincibility, Kyle suddenly snapped out of the Force speed. He lost his senses after a [[Concussion grenade/Legends|concussion grenade]] explosion, and was slumped against the elevator with the three remaining bounty hunters approaching him. Kyle didn't respond to the radio and Jan moved towards the flashes of light and the explosion, activating the ''Crow''{{'s}} weapons. The bounty hunters who turned around when they heard the ship approaching, attempted to shoot their blasters but the ''Crow'' evaded them and swung firing the cannon, killing them. The remaining bounty hunter alive decided to continue playing dead when Ors exited the ship armed with two blasters. She saw that Kyle was still alive and dragged him up the ramp. ==Aftermath== After three [[Standard day/Legends|days']] transit, Ors brought Katarn, his health fluctuating, to the [[Hospital ship/Legends|hospital ship]] [[Mercy (hospital ship)|''Mercy'']] stationed with the ''[[New Hope]]'', a [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]] [[Dreadnaught-class heavy cruiser/Legends|Dreadnaught]], in the [[Milagro system/Legends|Milagro system]]; while Katarn was healing in a [[Bacta tank/Legends|bacta tank]] Ors stole the mysterious holodisk to find out what was so important for him. On the ''New Hope'' she managed, with the help of [[Technician/Legends|technicians]] [[Xiong Wong]] and [[Wires]], to decrypt the holodisc, playing back the low-quality recording to her superiors: [[Jedi Knight/Legends|Jedi Knight]] [[Luke Skywalker/Legends|Luke Skywalker]], [[Princess/Legends|Princess]] [[Leia Organa Solo/Legends|Leia Organa]], and former [[Senator/Legends|Senator]] [[Mon Mothma/Legends|Mon Mothma]]. Following Skywalker's advice, she never told Katarn that she knew the content of the holodisc. Katarn and Ors then set off to the [[Katarn family compound]] on [[Sulon/Legends|Sulon]] to find a way of decrypting the disc and learning more of Morgan Katarn's killers. ==Appearances== *''[[Dark Forces: Rebel Agent]]'' *[[Dark Forces: Rebel Agent (audio drama)|''Dark Forces: Rebel Agent'' audio drama]] *''[[Star Wars: Jedi Knight: Dark Forces II]]'' {{1st}} ==Sources== *''[[Star Wars: Jedi Knight: Dark Forces II: The Official Strategy Guide]]'' *{{Wizards|url=default.asp?x=swminis/article/kylekatarnstale4|series=Kyle Katarn's Tale|text=Mission 4}} *''[[The Essential Reader's Companion]]'' ==Notes and references== {{Reflist}} {{GCW Valley}} {{Interlang |de=Mission von Nar Shaddaa (5 NSY) |es=Misión a Nar Shaddaa (5 DBY) }} {{DEFAULTSORT:Nar Shaddaa (5 ABY), mission to}} [[Category:Battles of the Search for the Valley of the Jedi]] [[Category:Missions to Nar Shaddaa]]
